data.title: Tesla: Strong Q3, But Beware The Q4 Slump
data.description:
Article Thesis

Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) reported its delivery numbers for the third quarter on Thursday, beating estimates with a strong showing. But that was likely due to one-time effects—the $7,500 tax credit that expired at the end of September pulled sales forward, which is why Q4 will likely be significantly weaker. While the compelling deliveries during the third quarter will likely result in better profitability and cash flow reported for that quarter, Tesla's automobile business, overall, still doesn't look too strong right now.

Past Coverage

I have written about Tesla here on Seeking Alpha in the past, with this being my most recent article on the company. Since Tesla reported its Q3 deliveries on Thursday, with the company beating estimates easily, it is time to take another look at the company today -- what does the strong delivery number for the third quarter mean for TSLA's upcoming Q3 report, and what does it mean for demand in the fourth quarter?

What Happened?

On Thursday morning, Tesla released the following Q3 delivery numbers:

We see that deliveries totaled 497,000 for the quarter, which was a new record—TSLA had never before delivered this many vehicles during a single 3-month span. The market didn't react positively to this news, however, as TSLA is down by 1%-2% at the time of writing -- despite the fact that deliveries were considerably higher than what the analyst community had expected, as the consensus estimate stood at 448,000. Tesla thus beat the consensus estimate by around 11%, which is a pretty sizeable beat. I believe that the lack of a positive market reaction may be caused by a combination of positive news already being priced into the stock, as TSLA is up quite a lot over the last month, while investors may also worry about demand in Q4, as I'll explain later in this article.

While that is a much smaller part of the business, Tesla also announced the deployment of 12.5 GWh of energy storage, which was a quarterly record as well.

Tesla's Q3: Strong Sales Volumes

I'm not a Tesla bull, but credit is due where it is due: Tesla had a strong quarter delivery-wise. With record volumes for both vehicles and energy storage, Tesla has stopped the operational declines during the third quarter. The fact that Tesla outperformed what analysts had been forecasting is also positive, with the beat of the consensus estimate being noteworthy.

On a year-over-year basis, Tesla grew its vehicle deliveries by 7%, from 463,000 vehicles during 2024's third quarter. That is, of course, pretty far away from the 50% growth rate CEO Elon Musk has guided for in the past, but nevertheless, Tesla's growth in Q3 was positive while many analysts had predicted a small decline. Relative to H1, the year-over-year performance during the third quarter was better as well.

Looking at the product mix, we see that it shifted even more to the volume models 3 and Y, which made up 97% of total deliveries, versus 95% in last year's third quarter. In other words, all other models declined from 5% of the total to just 3% of the total. This is bad news, I believe, as Tesla's non-3/Y models, i.e., the S, X, and Cybertruck, are higher-priced on average. Selling fewer expensive vehicles and more cheap ones is bad for average selling prices and margins, all else equal. The steep decline in the non-3/Y models also suggests that the Cybertruck continues to perform way worse compared to Tesla's goals and Elon Musk's estimates—it is, I would say, a failed venture.

Tesla also released its production volumes for the third quarter, with 447,000 vehicles being produced during Q3. That was a bit less than one year earlier, when 470,000 vehicles were produced. This means that while Tesla produced more vehicles than it sold in last year's third quarter (and in this year's H1), the company sold more vehicles than it produced in the most recent quarter.

This could theoretically be the result of production constraints, but I believe that it is more likely that the company kept production at a somewhat lower rate on purpose to reduce its inventory. After all, reducing the inventory, which had grown in recent quarters, is good for both Tesla's cash flow as well as for its balance sheet—when inventory levels decline, cash is freed up and free cash flow and net cash look better, all else equal. Since free cash flow generation has been bad in recent quarters, partially due to growing inventory levels, it is opportune to reverse this trend by keeping production levels below the sales pace for a while.

Looking at Tesla's energy business, which contributed around 12% of overall revenue during the second quarter, we see a strong volume growth rate: Deployment totaled 12.5 GWh, versus 6.9 GWh one year earlier. That makes for a hefty 81% growth rate, although from a relatively low level, as Q3 2024 had not been a strong quarter—Q2 2024, for example, had a deployment level of 9.4 GWh. The energy business has always been a bit lumpy, so the year-over-year growth rate during a single quarter isn't always telling a lot, but the fact that this was the best quarter on record is still a major positive, of course—the previous record had been set in Q4 2024, when TSLA deployed 11 GWh of energy storage. The revenue growth rate of the energy business had been negative during the second quarter, at -7%. Thus, the record deployment in Q3 and the strong year-over-year growth rate are very welcome—the company will most likely report a nice revenue increase for the third quarter when it reports its financial results at the end of October.

The $7,500 Tax Credit And Demand Being Pulled Forward

Tesla's year-over-year growth rate in terms of vehicle deliveries was not strong, I think, considering its high valuation, but it was still positive and a record quarter. Unfortunately, that will likely not repeat in Q4, as a lot of demand was pulled forward by a one-time effect: The $7,500 tax credit in the U.S. for electric vehicles ended on September 30; thus, it is no longer available today. That was, of course, known to everyone who was generally interested in buying an electric vehicle. Electric vehicle sales thus got a nice boost during the third quarter, as EV buyers decided to get a vehicle a little early in order to get the tax credit. This was not a Tesla-specific item but rather something that was felt by other automobile companies, too: Ford Motor Company (F), for example, saw its Mustang Mach-E sales jump by more than 50% during the third quarter, while General Motors Company (GM) saw its electric vehicle sales soar by an extraordinary 107%.

In other words, Q3 was a strong quarter for basically everyone who sold EVs in the U.S., as a lot of demand was pulled forward. This, of course, means that this demand is no longer there in Q4—those who bought an EV in August or September won't buy another one in October. With the $7,500 tax credit no longer being available, EVs also got more expensive relative to ICE vehicles, all else equal, making them less competitive. I thus believe that Q4 will show rather weak EV sales numbers for all players—the impact will be felt most by those that do not sell ICE vehicles but that are EV pure-plays, of course.

Is TSLA A Good Investment Following The Q3 Deliveries Beat?

I believe that the answer to the above question is "No." Yes, Tesla beat estimates, and Tesla showed some growth. But 7% volume growth with a weak product mix isn't all that good, especially during a period when competitors show much better EV sales growth thanks to one-time political tailwinds from the tax credit pulling demand forward. Tesla wanted to grow by 50% per year, and it is priced like an extremely fast-growing company, trading for more than 270x this year's earnings per share.

7% volume growth is, I believe, not good enough at all to justify a Buy rating under these conditions. Tesla may ultimately be successful with its robot ventures and other emerging businesses, but that is far from guaranteed, and at a $1.5 trillion market capitalization, a lot of success is already priced into the stock. I continue to see TSLA stock as overvalued.

data.title: Anavex: ANAVEX 3-71 Could Potentially Offer Disease-Modifying Effects For Schizophrenia
data.description:
Anavex Life Sciences Overview

The last time I spoke about Anavex Life Sciences Corp. (NASDAQ:AVXL) just released positive results from its phase 2 ANAVEX3-71-SZ-001 study using ANAVEX 3-71 for the treatment of patients with schizophrenia on stable antipsychotics. As I will go over below, the company saw improved biomarker trends over placebo as an encouraging sign and believes that this could be offered up as a disease-modifying therapy to treat these patients with this neuropsychiatric disorder. There were two other positive items of note released from this trial data that could make it a competitive oral drug for these patients. The first of which being that the primary endpoint of safety was established. This is in terms of Part A and Part B of this phase 2 trial.

The point is that there were no serious or severe treatment-emergent adverse events [TEAEs] to take note of. The second item that was released alongside this trial data of safety and biomarker trends was that of successfully modifying ANAVEX 3-71 as a once-daily oral tablet to treat these patients with schizophrenia. To that end, it is moving away from an immediate-release oral capsule to a modified release. The end result being that the pharmacokinetic profile of this modified version of ANAVEX 3-71 was superior to its counterpart. If anything, there is one major takeaway from this specific drug molecule in clinical development, which is that it offers up a holistic approach to treating patients with schizophrenia. Instead of only targeting one of three aspects of this disorder, it is able to modify all three.

These aspects observed in it are positive symptoms, negative symptoms, and cognitive function. This gives Anavex the ability to be competitive in this specific market segment. As far as the European review of ANAVEX 2-73 [blarcamesine], the Marketing Authorization Application [MAA] of it was accepted by the European Medicines Agency [EMA] in December of 2024. The MAA is expected to give guidance or an opinion on this application to the European Commission [EC], who is then to make a final decision if this should be approved to treat patients with Alzheimer's Disease [AD].

Anavex 3-71 Biomarker Trend For Reducing Neuroinflammation Is Encouraging

As I noted above, the company released positive data from its phase 2 ANAVEX3-71-SZ-001 study using its candidate ANAVEX 3-71 for the treatment of patients with schizophrenia. The goal was to evaluate this dual sigma1 receptor agonist and M1 muscarinic receptor for the treatment of these patients. This was in terms of safety, pharmacokinetic profile, and biomarker evaluation as well. Before going over this mid-stage trial data in detail, it is first important to note what this disorder is and what the possible market opportunity for the company could end up being. Schizophrenia is characterized as a disorder by which a person's thinking, feelings, and behavior are all altered. Furthermore, this disorder is broken down into three domains, and they are as follows:

  • Positive symptoms
  • Negative symptoms
  • Cognitive issues

In terms of positive symptoms, it could be something like hallucinations or delusions coming about. Even behavior changes such as acting in a different manner when in social situations. On the other hand, negative symptoms of schizophrenia deal with flat emotion, inability to feel pleasure and not enough motivation being present. The cognitive function would include items such as not being able to complete daily tasks and memory issues. The global schizophrenia drugs market size is expected to reach $11.19 billion by 2030. This is a very large market and I believe that the company has a good shot at targeting it because of the versatility that its drug offers. Being that it is a dual sigma-1 receptor agonist and M1 muscarinic receptor, it is able to take a fully targeted approach to treating these schizophrenia patients.

In order to determine if the company is going to be successful in using ANAVEX 3-71 for the treatment of patients with schizophrenia on stable antipsychotics, it is in the process of the ongoing phase 2 ANAVEX3-71-SZ-001 study. This particular mid-stage trial is expected to recruit up to a total of 71 patients who are to be randomized to receive either drug or placebo. It is important to highlight right away that this specific study incorporated two specific parts, and they were as follows:

  • Part A - was looking at various initial aspects of testing like the pharmacokinetic profile of ANAVEX 3-71, Multiple ascending doses [MADs] and safety
  • Part B - This is where the randomized, double-blind, placebo controlled portion comes into play

The primary endpoint was just looking for safety and like in prior healthy volunteer studies it had completed, there were no serious or severe treatment-emergent adverse events [TEAEs] noted. The company, though, has observed a huge positive and this is with respect to a biomarker of clinical testing it was able to establish with the patients who took ANAVEX 3-71. Specifically, it was shown that it was able to reduce a biomarker of neuroinflammation known as glial fibrillary acidic protein [GFAP] compared to that of placebo. Not only was this observed, but it was stated that with extended treatment duration there could be a dose-dependent effect of improvement. Lastly, the whole goal of oral ANAVEX 3-71 is to have a disease-modifying impact on schizophrenia. In my opinion, this drug is already ahead of the game, offering a novel mode of action. Furthermore, it goes beyond this in that it is able to target all three domains for these patients. These would be the three domains I listed above of: Positive symptoms, negative symptoms, and cognitive functioning. Other biomarkers of positive outcomes observed as trends were electroencephalography or EEG [brain activity measures when tasks are being done to look for abnormal patterns] and event-related potential or ERP [sensory or cognitive testing]. It is important to highlight that everything has been observed with positive trends in favor of ANAVEX 3-71 and thus longer duration testing over an extended period of time will need to be observed for further confirmation of drug activity in targeting these schizophrenia patients.

Beyond the scope of just seeing if ANAVEX 3-71 is effective, it had decided to modify its drug to have a superior pharmacokinetic profile in patients. To that end, it initially was looking towards having an immediate-release oral tablet formulation for these patients. However, further testing showed that it could do better for them with a modified-release oral version of this drug. This is a good move, because with this type of oral release of drug ANAVEX 3-71 it is going to be able to achieve once-daily dosing for patients, which would be far more convenient than other oral dosing options. Besides a once-daily version of this drug achieving greater patient compliance, it was also established in such a manner as to be competitive against other drugs being developed to treat patients with schizophrenia.

AVXL Stock Financials

According to the 10-Q SEC Filing, Anavex Life Sciences had cash and cash equivalents of $101.2 million as of June 30, 2025. The truth is that this company is in excellent shape when it comes to its cash runway. This is not based on my estimate, but instead what the company noted in its Q3 of 2025 financial and business update report. It believes that the cash on hand it has is likely going to be enough to fund its operations for at least 3 years. Even if this biotech does need to raise funds somehow, it has a few options already in place to utilize. The first of such options would be in terms of a 2023 purchase agreement it had made with Lincoln Park Capital Fund, LLC.

This particular deal established that Anavex from time to time could sell up to a total of $150 million worth of shares of its common stock. There are a few stipulations to note, though, which is important with respect to this financial tool. The first is that this agreement is only set in stone for a 3-year period until February 3, 2026. Secondly, in order for the company to tap into the remaining $110.8 million, it is going to have to file a prospectus supplement. The second option it has in place would be in terms of a Sales Agreement it entered into with TD Securities LLC, where it could offer and sell up to an aggregate of $150 million from time to time. The company burns roughly $14.5 million of cash per quarter. This is broken down into $10 million in R&D expenses and then $4.5 million in G&A expenses.

Risks To Business

There are several risks that investors should be aware of before investing in Anavex Life Sciences. The first risk to consider would be in terms of the latest data released from the phase 2 ANAVEX3-71-SZ-001 study using ANAVEX 3-71 for the treatment of patients with schizophrenia. First and foremost, the primary endpoint of safety was achieved and it was shown that there were positive trends of biomarkers observed for the patients who took Anavex's drug. The risk here is that once this program moves towards phase 3 testing or beyond, there is no assurance that no serious or severe treatment-emergent adverse event [TEAE] is not going to pop up. The second risk is in terms of the biomarkers observed thus far, especially GFAP, where ANAVEX 3-71 had a more favorable trend in this regard. The risk here is that these effects observed on biomarkers, were just that, trends. There is no assurance that the final outcome is going to show this drug to achieve statistical significance compared to GFAP or any other biomarker of schizophrenia in late-stage testing.

The second risk to consider would be in terms of the submission of the Marketing Authorization Application [MAA] of ANAVEX 2-73 [blarcamesine] for the treatment of patients with AD to the European Medicines Agency [EMA]. The EMA is expected to provide an opinion of the MAA to the EC and then this body is going to make a final determination of whether or not this sigma-1 receptor agonist should be approved to treat these patients with this neurodegenerative disorder. The risk here is that there is no assurance that the EMA is going to issue a positive opinion of this regulatory application. The second risk then would be that there is no guarantee that the EC is going to end up giving full approval of blarcamesine to treat patients with AD.

The third and final risk to consider would actually be the potential expansion opportunities for ANAVEX 3-71. That's because this drug provides a dual role of agonism of the sigma-1 receptor and M1 muscarinic receptor and could end up being a more potent drug to treat both neurodegenerative and neuropsychiatric disorders. With that being said, the hope is to expand the use of this particular drug to treat patients with AD and frontotemporal dementia [FTD]. The risk here is that even though ANAVEX 3-71 was shown to do well in terms of trends of improvement of biomarkers of schizophrenia, there is no guarantee that it will be able to achieve a similar or superior outcome when targeting these other two indications.

Conclusion

Anavex Life Sciences continues to do well in advancing its drug ANAVEX 3-71 for the treatment of patients with schizophrenia. That's because it has noted some positive trends in biomarkers that relate to this disorder in the ongoing phase 2 ANAVEX 3-71-SZ-001 study. A good thing about this drug is that it has a holistic approach to targeting this disorder and it is because of the development for it to target all three domains. Hopefully it will have the EMA issue a positive opinion to the EC of blarcamesine for the treatment of patients with AD.

However, this would just be an opinion, and the ultimate final decision of whether or not this drug is to be marketed in the EU to treat these patients will be decided upon by the EC itself. The company is already in good shape because ANAVEX 3-71 offers an additional shot on goal in targeting AD patients. With positive data released from the phase 2 ANAVEX3-71-SZ-001 study of ANAVEX 3-71 to target patients with schizophrenia, plus the EC decision expected to come about for potential marketing of blarcamesine for the treatment of patients with AD, I believe that investors could benefit from any potential gains made here.

data.title: Axcelis And Veeco: Interested In This Tie-Up
data.description:
Shares of Axcelis Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:ACLS) have been in the spotlight this week as the company announced a merger with Veeco Instruments Inc. (NASDAQ:VECO) in order to gain scale and capabilities in a rapidly evolving semiconductor supplier landscape.

I can understand the small negative share price reaction of shares of Axcelis given the relative premium offered in the exchange ratio of the shares in the new combination. Overall, the valuation looks rather compelling, as the merged business looks to enjoy a stronger market position if all goes to plan.

Foundational Technology For Semiconductor Manufacturing

Axcelis operates in the so-called ION implementation phase in semiconductor production, right in between lithography and etch, with ION implementation being a key step to meet device functionality and performance.

In this process, materials are delivered that locally change properties of semiconductors, thereby modifying electrical or physical characteristics of materials to impact their performance. Crucial in this process is to deliver ions with purity in the right dose, energy and angle.

Axcelis plays the role of ion implanter with its implant technology, which requires the scale of semiconductor manufacturing to be combined with the precision of nuclear physics. Such solutions carry over 10,000 individual parts, bolstering incomprehensible numbers of lines of code and particles/ions involved.

The company generated around a billion in revenues in 2024 from the production and sale of such devices and services, catering to segments that largely relate to general mature and silicon carbide. Notably, electrification, AI and automotive offer huge growth segments.

The company has seen quite an interesting organic growth trajectory over the past decade, having essentially tripled sales in just ten years' time, while growing operating margins to the high teens, even near the 20%, all while minimal dilution has been incurred.

All this has been reflected in the share price, and perhaps some more, as a $10 stock in 2015 peaked around the $200 mark in 2023, and after seeing a pullback to the $50 mark in spring, shares have recovered a long way to the $100 mark here.

A Tie-Up

On the first day of October, Axcelis and Veeco Instruments announced their intentions to combine operations in an all-stock merger valued at $4.4 billion. Deal terms call for 0.3575 shares in Axcelis granted to investors in Veeco (for every share they own), resulting in a 58/42% pro forma shareholder distribution, with Axcelis being, of course, a larger firm as well.

The deal is driven by greater scale, firm capabilities, larger addressable markets, diversification in the portfolio and complementary expertise and innovation power. This combines Axcelis' strength in ion, complemented by Veeco's strength in wet processing, annealing, MOCVD, and Ion Beams.

Real cost savings are anticipated as well, seen at $35 million some 24 months following closure, equal to 2% of the pro forma revenue base of around $1.7 billion.

Talking about the pro forma implications, the merger is seen posting revenues at $1.7 billion per annum with adjusted EBITDA reported at $387 million, that is excluding the anticipated synergies for good order. Veeco generates 41% of pro forma sales, yet its EBITDA contribution is only seen at 35% of the combination, given its lower margins, as the exchange ratio looks favorable to investors in Veeco in that light.

As of the most recent quarter, Axcelis operates with about 32 million shares outstanding, valuing the business around $3.1 billion at $97 per share, whoever including a more than half a billion net cash position.

The 60 million shares of Veeco were valued at around $1.8 billion based on an unaffected share price around the $30 mark, including a much more modest net cash position, giving its shareholders relatively more value in this deal.

Shares of Veeco rose some 10% in exchange for the merger of equals, which is also really an acquisition, as shares of Axcelis fell some 3%. This suggests that a roughly $200 million premium for Veeco is offset by a roughly similar decline in value of shares of Axcelis (including the to be issued shares) despite the promise of synergies.

What Now?

Frankly, the valuation looks quite interesting here, although Veeco is awarded a slightly higher multiple. A $4.4 billion transaction price is based on the valuation of the operating assets, which are valued at around 2.5 times sales and just over 11 times EBITDA, and at 10 times and change EBITDA multiple if we factor in synergies.

Putting some numbers into perspective, Axcelis reported net income of $200 million in 2024, which is a clean GAAP number not impacted by stock-based compensation expense, although benefiting modestly from interest on net cash holdings, of course. Based on a share count of some 32 million shares, that works down to earnings of $6.25 per share.

Net earnings of Veeco were reported at $73 million, although impacted by a net income of $13 million after certain charges. Pro forma all this, net earnings could be seen around $286 million, and with a pro forma share count seen around 55 million shares, earnings would come in a dollar lower, around $5.20 per share, due to the unfavorable exchange ratio with Veeco's investors given an above average share in the new combination.

A $35 million synergy target could boost pre-tax earnings by more than sixty cents ahead of taxes, not making up for the dilution, contrary to the claim of management, which believes that the deal will be accretive in the first year upon closing.

In either case, earnings are seen trending over $6 per share as the company operates with a pro forma net cash position exceeding $10 per share. At a current share price around $95 per share, this results in an operating asset valuation of $85 per share. Based on an earnings number around $6 per share, multiples look very compelling at just 14-15 times for a well-positioned business that operates with a strong net cash position.

All this means that I want to learn much more about the business and the combination and its prospects going forward, with a full intention to build conviction to get involved here.

data.title: Why AST SpaceMobile Soared (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
On October 1, 2025, AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (NASDAQ:ASTS) broke out of its sub-$50 support price, gaining over 15% on the day. At the time of writing, ASTS stock is adding another 10% and trading at around $62. The communications equipment supplier popped after the firm announced that it had completed the final assembly of BlueBird 6. In response, an analyst at Barclays raised the price target to $60, up from $37.

Incidentally, the analyst downgraded Viasat, Inc. (VSAT), whose stock I covered in January. VSAT stock gained 191% in that time. In 2025, ASTS' stock more than doubled, outpacing Viasat’s return.

BlueBird Satellite Production and Launch Updates

AST SpaceMobile surged on Wednesday and today after it announced expectations of 45-60 BlueBird satellites in orbit by the end of next year. On October 12, the company will ship BlueBird 6 to India.

According to the company, the next generation launch campaign highlights are as follows:

  • BlueBird 7 is expected to ship to the Cape Canaveral launch site in October.
  • BlueBird's 8-16 has a variable production stage schedule, with launches planned every 1-2 months on average this year and next.
  • AST is on track to complete 40 phased arrays by early 2026 (BlueBird 46).
  • Expect between 45-60 satellites in orbit by year-end 2026.

AST also posted on X (formerly Twitter) that it partnered with more than 50 mobile network operators. That provides a total addressable market of around three billion subscribers. Customers benefit from AST’s flexible spectrum strategy since they will have access to its licensed spectrum along with that of the partner.

The BlueBirds will have the title of the largest commercial satellites deployed in LEO. The 2,400 sq ft phased array supports direct-to-cell broadband. The processing bandwidth is up to 10,000 MHz, while the peak 5G speed is 120 Mbps per cell.

Relief Rally Explained

Between July and early September, ASTS stock slipped from $60 to below $40. At the time, short sellers, who hold a 20.2% short float against the stock, were in control. They likely bet too confidently on AST missing its targeted satellite build and launch dates. The BlueBird timeline removes those uncertainties. As a result, ASTS stock is trading at an all-time high. At a market capitalization of $22.9 billion, it is far larger than Viasat at $4.1 billion. Conversely, T-Mobile US, Inc. (TMUS) is over 10 times bigger. Still, investors would hold larger firms like AT&T Inc. (T) or Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ) to collect dividend income.

Within its peer group, ASTS stock has the largest market capitalization. Both Lumen Technologies, Inc. (LUMN), which I covered before, and Globalstar, Inc. (GSAT) are around one-quarter the size.

Seeking Alpha’s quant grades show LUMN and VSAT stock in value territory. All four firms, however, score an A- or higher on momentum. For now, these stocks benefit from positive sentiment.

Risks

AST SpaceMobile previously announced launch dates but then delayed them. One site questioned if its launch schedule was a pipedream. Readers will notice that AST’s X post on Sept. 30 mentions what investors already know about the launch schedule. For example, markets anticipated a lift-off schedule averaging one to two months, with 45 to 60 birds in orbit by the end of 2026.

The current ASTS stock rally might peak near its previous summer high in the $55–$60 range.

Your Takeaway

AST SpaceMobile had a history of breaking out between 2021 and 2024 before fading. That changed in mid-2024 as investors built a position during the firm’s pre-commercial revenue phase. In January, the stock dipped by 11% despite its partnerships with AT&T and Verizon. Since its “buy” rating then, the stock has more than doubled.

The market is demonstrating renewed confidence in the success of the satellite launches through next year, suggesting that the breakout will continue. Short-sellers will need to consider covering their position to avoid further losses.

data.title: IVW: S&P 500 Growth For Tactical And Long-Term Investors
data.description:
The iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF (NYSEARCA:IVW) is a passively managed exchange-traded fund [ETF] designed to provide investors with exposure to large-cap growth companies. IVW is a low-cost strategy with an expense ratio of 18 bps, making this an appealing investment vehicle for those seeking growth exposure.

About iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF

IVW was launched on May 22, 2000, by BlackRock Fund Advisors with iShares on the NYSE Arca exchange. The strategy is passively managed and is indexed to the S&P 500 Growth Index. IVW provides investors with substantial depth and liquidity with roughly $66b in assets under management [AUM] with an average of $244.5mm in share value changing hands on a daily basis. The strategy pays out a modest dividend of $0.50/share, yielding 0.41% on a trailing twelve-month basis.

The S&P 500 Growth Index is managed by S&P Dow Jones Indices, a division of S&P Global. The Index uses a modified market-cap weighted approach when selecting constituents and is rebalanced quarterly. The index tracks 216 constituents with a total market cap of $42T. The Index uses various growth and valuation methods when considering constituents to be included in the strategy, including 3-year earnings per share as a yield over the price-per-share, the 3-year sales per share growth rate, and momentum. Stocks must also have a market cap of at least $20.5b in order to be included in the Index. In total, the S&P 500 Index represents roughly 51.67% of the Parent Index.

The Index caps the weight of any single constituent at 23%; the sum of constituents above 4.8% must not exceed an aggregate weight of 50% of the Index.

IVW is heavily weighted in the information technology and communications sectors, making up 42.54% and 15.37% of the total portfolio weight, respectively. As a concentrated component of the S&P 500, IVW holds 216 individual stocks with a portfolio price/earnings [P/E] ratio of 39.05x. The strategy has a relatively moderate 3-year equity beta of 1.06x, suggesting that IVW can be expected to exhibit slightly higher volatility when compared to the broader Index.

The strategy is relatively concentrated with the top 10 holdings making up 54.75% of the total portfolio weight. In contrast, the bottom 10 holdings comprise a mere 0.18% of the portfolio weight. Top holdings include many of the mega-cap technology companies like NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) at 14.59%, Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) at 6.42%, Apple Inc. (AAPL) at 5.57%, and Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) at 5.11%.

Looking through the top holdings across sectors, it’s clear that technology and communications are prioritized in terms of weight. I believe this factor is most relevant to investors when constructing a diversified equity strategy, particularly when considering exposure across companies and sectors. For example, an investor should not expect to gain a significant amount of exposure to the energy, utilities, materials, or real estate sectors.

In terms of growth drivers for IVW, the top 10 holdings will have the largest impact on the direction of the portfolio strategy. This may result in a singular, AI-oriented theme for the strategy to a high degree, particularly as high-weighted stocks like Nvidia, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Broadcom Inc. (AVGO), Alphabet Inc. (GOOG) (GOOGL), and Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) are heavily dependent on AI infrastructure investments to drive top-line growth. At a high level, continued capital investments in AI infrastructure across the cloud service providers [CSPs] will drive revenue growth, allowing these organizations to convert their growing backlogs into revenue. In turn, this will drive revenue growth for AI accelerator and network equipment developers like Nvidia and Broadcom.

Who Should Consider IVW as an Investment Strategy

IVW can be an appealing equity investment strategy for investors seeking exposure to information technology and communications, and to a lesser degree, broad exposure to other sectors. Investors can utilize IVW for tactical positioning or for long-term secular growth. Using the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) for comparison purposes, investors can expect IVW to provide more exposure to capital growth while SPY may blend equities that may be considered as income or value stocks alongside growth stocks.

Considering peer strategies, investors may consider the SPDR® Portfolio S&P 500 Growth ETF (SPYG) and the Vanguard S&P 500 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VOOG) as low-cost alternatives to IVW. SPYG and VOOG have a relatively lower fee of 4 bps and 7 bps, respectively. This compares to the 18 bps fee IVW commands.

Comparing performance, each of these strategies performs relatively similarly within a few percentage points over time. If selecting an ETF with the S&P 500 growth theme, I’m selecting the lowest-cost ETF given the similar performance expectations.

Risks Related to IVW

IVW is a passively managed, indexed ETF, exposing investors to certain risks that must be considered prior to making a final investment decision. Indexed strategies mean that the portfolio will not aim to beat the underlying Index and will not deviate from the strategy during periods of market stress. As a result of this, investors will be responsible for actively managing their holdings and risk.

IVW exhibits significant sector and stock concentration, heavily weighted towards the information technology and communications sectors with a high concentration towards the top 10 holdings. Given that many indexed funds, along with many other equity funds may heavily weight these stocks, investors should consider potential overlap across their portfolio holdings to ensure their portfolios are adequately diversified.

Final Thoughts

IVW is an indexed investment strategy designed to provide investors with exposure to growth-oriented stocks within the S&P 500. The strategy can be used for growth equity exposure as well as exposure to the information technology and communications sectors.

This article answers these three main questions about IVW:

  • How does IVW compare to other growth ETFs in terms of expenses and performance?
  • Are there concentration concerns with an investment in IVW?
  • Is IVW a good fund for providing exposure to the growth segment of the market?

Editor's Note: This article is intended to provide a general overview of the ETF for educational purposes only and, unlike other articles on Seeking Alpha, does not offer an investment opinion about the ETF.

data.title: Wesdome Gold Mines: Where High-Grade Ounces Meet Top-Ranked Jurisdictions
data.description:
All figures are in United States Dollars unless otherwise noted with a C$ or A$ in front of the figure. G/T = grams per tonne (of gold or silver). GEOs = gold-equivalent ounces. SEOs = silver-equivalent ounces. AISC refers to all-in sustaining costs. LOMP = life of mine plan. TPD = tonnes per day. UG = Underground. MTPA = million tonnes per annum. FS/DFS = Definitive Feasibility Study.

Q2 Production & Sales

Wesdome Gold Mines Ltd. (OTCQX:WDOFF) released its Q2 '25 results in August, reporting quarterly production of ~42,800 ounces of gold, a 3% decline from the year-ago period. Lower production was related to a much softer quarter at Kiena in Quebec (~17,200 ounces vs. ~24,800 ounces), offsetting a better quarter at its Eagle River Mine in Ontario where output increased 33% year-over-year. Unfortunately, this has left Wesdome at risk of missing its initial guidance midpoint of 200,000 ounces, with just ~88,500 ounces year-to-date or just ~44% of its guidance midpoint.

This is unfortunate, given that the company was coming off two consecutive modest beats on its guidance midpoint in 2023 and 2024 under new leadership, with 2025 setting up to be a minor miss without a very strong performance out of Kiena in Q4 '25.

Digging into its operations a little closer, Eagle River put together a solid performance in Q2 '25, producing ~25,600 ounces at $1,394/oz AISC, up from ~19,300 ounces at $1,860/oz in the year-ago period. Increased production was driven by industry-leading grades of 16.9 G/T of gold (Q2 '24: 11.8 G/T of gold), as well as higher recoveries at 96.7%, with Wesdome noting that grades benefited from improved dilution control. Notably, these results were achieved despite a planned 18-day mill shut in Q2'25 that impacted throughput in the period.

Given the strong performance in H1'25 (~54,600 ounces), Wesdome increased Eagle River's guidance midpoint by 5,000 ounces to 110,000 ounces while also increasing guidance on grades (14.0 - 15.0 G/T of gold vs. 13.0 - 15.0 G/T of gold for year previously). And while the year-to-date grade of 16.2 G/T of gold implies lower grades in H2 '25, operations will benefit from increased throughput relative to Q2 '25 levels. As for the impressive performance that bet plans in H1, Wesdome called out higher grade reconciliation and increased ounces from the 300 Zone, benefiting from optimized stope design, improved execution and refined grade control.

As for cost performance at Eagle River, lower AISC ($1,394/oz vs. $1,860/oz) benefited from increased ounces sold vs. produced offset by higher sustaining capital and higher royalties. One of the more important take-aways from the quarter was below, with Wesdome stating that the better performance at Eagle River was largely execution driven, not chasing grades, an encouraging sign that optimization work here and investments in the asset are paying off.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

"Importantly, performance year-to-date reflects disciplined execution, not opportunistic grade chasing."

- Wesdome Q2 '25 Conference Call.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Unfortunately, while investors couldn't have asked for a much better H1 out of Eagle River, Kiena materially underperformed. This was evidenced by production of just ~17,200 ounces at $1,720/oz AISC in Q2'25, down from ~24,800 ounces at $1,123/oz AISC. Lower production in the period was related to significantly lower grades (10.7 G/T of gold vs. 13.5 G/T of gold), lower recoveries and lower mill throughput. Wesdome noted that equipment constraints and limited access to stopes and underperformance in one high-grade stope were responsible for the weaker results.

Given the weak Q2 '25 performance combined with an already weak Q1 '25, Kiena is sitting at just ~33,900 ounces year-to-date at $1,567/oz AISC, a considerable deviation from original guidance of 95,000 ounces at $1,312/oz AISC using the midpoint. And with the asset tracking this far behind plans, the company chose to revise guidance lower to 80,000 to 90,000 ounces at higher costs of $1,488/oz.

As for tackling these issues, the company is adjusting its maintenance practices to better align with Kiena Deep's current risk profile, with it stating that an optimized shift schedule will ensure round-the-clock maintenance coverage. In addition to this, rental equipment has been brought in to improve its fleet and reduce the risk of downtime, and it has increased its spare parts inventory. Finally, Wesdome shared that it is bringing more people to critical areas of the mine to build redundancy and has opened an additional maintenance workshop underground.

The latter should allow for better equipment utilization rates and reduce the impact to the mining sequence, which impacted Q2 '25 results because of higher-grade stopes being deferred into the second half. On a negative note, Q3 '25 didn't start as cleanly as hoped with a planned 4-day shutdown for hoist maintenance morphed into ten days of downtime, impacting mine production.

While these results may not be very inspiring, nor is the guidance cut, there is reason to be optimistic about this asset's future and why it makes sense to ignore the weak H1 '25 results. For starters, Wesdome noted it expects a much better Q4 with enhanced maintenance practices, an increase in available mining horizons and recent changes to site leadership. And it's important to remember that Kiena is currently impacted by having a single mining area exacerbating any issues given the lack of operational flexibility.

Fortunately, Wesdome will have three mining horizons available by year-end. This will triple its available mining areas, with these new areas including Presqu'ile and Level 136. Not only will this translate to a material increase in mine production, but it will greatly improve operational flexibility, with a single stope or two not making or breaking its quarter as we saw in Q2 '25. Just as importantly, Wesdome will see a significant improvement in its materials handling with the Presqu'ile ramp, providing a second means of transportation for people and materials vs. solely its shaft currently.

These improvements, in addition to increased ventilation and welcome surprises from an exploration standpoint (like much higher grades than anticipated at the B Zone), are positioning this asset to materially outperform on output relative to its LOMP delivered to the market in 2021. For those unfamiliar, this mine plan pointed to average annual production of ~84,000 ounces per year over seven years, with peak production of ~120,000 ounces.

Under the current setup, Presqu'ile alone looks like it can add up to 30,000 ounces/year of incremental ounces. However, Presqu'ile and Kiena Deep will still only be utilizing ~60% of the 2,000 TPD permitted capacity at the mill, with Wesdome's larger goal being to get both mills operating at 80% of capacity, implying rates closer to 1,600 TPD for Kiena. And with no shortage of targets at depth and others accessible from the 33 Level exploration ramp, there's certainly the potential for this to be a 170,000 plus ounce per annum asset long-term, if not better.

As for progress at Presqu'ile, this new near-surface mine is expected to contribute up to 10,000 ounces this year and drilling of the first stopes at Presqu'ile hit visible gold in areas of high-grade block model designs, an encouraging sign in regards to modeling completed here. On a negative note, initial stope production at Presqu'ile has been revised from "late 2025 or Q1 2026" vs. "by the end of 2025" previously. This is hardly a deal-breaker but is a minor change in schedule vs. prior expectations, suggesting that we may see slightly lower grades in Q1'26 vs. prior expectations for the larger Kiena Complex if Presqu'ile is still reliant on development ore for an extra couple of months.

Looking at the disappointing Q2 '25 cost performance with AISC spiking 53% year-over-year to $1,720/oz, this was related to much higher sustaining capital year-over-year (C$15.3 million vs. C$9.8 million) primarily related to increase exploration and mine capital equipment costs, and fewer ounces sold, with fixed costs divided over a much smaller denominator. However, Kiena should look much different in Q4 '25 as production ramps up and with the benefit of new ounces from Presqu'ile, leveraging its fixed cost base and bringing AISC back below $1,400/oz, with a further improvement in AISC next year as stope ore is available from Presqu'ile.

As for project costs, Wesdome noted that exploration ramp development and associated infrastructure is trending higher than budget, though partially related to scope expansion in order to increase ventilation capacity.

Financial Results

As for Wesdome's financial results, revenue jumped 63% year-over-year to ~C$209 million, operating cash flow soared to ~C$101 million (+77% year-over-year) and free cash flow increased to ~C$53 million despite increased capex spend year-over-year. Revenue and operating cash flow benefited from higher ounces sold than produced (~45,900 vs. ~42,800) and a record average realized gold price of $3,279/oz, while free cash flow benefited from a slight under-spend on capex year-to-date (C$80 million vs. C$190 million).

Fortunately, increased capex in the back half will be offset by higher production levels and continued strength in the gold price (XAUUSD:CUR), translating to another two very strong quarters of free cash flow ahead. As for Wesdome's balance sheet, it remains pristine despite the increased spending at both sites this year and C$33.5 million in cash related to the Angus Gold acquisition. In fact, Wesdome is sitting on ~C$188 million in cash and no debt. Notably, Wesdome increased its RCF to US$250 million with an additional US$50 million accordion, giving the over C$500 million in liquidity.

The last point worth noting is that Wesdome shared that its strong balance sheet has prompted it to look at a disciplined capital return to shareholders while balancing spend on exploration and growth, suggesting investors could see dividends or buybacks at some point in 2026. Assuming the company looked at paying a dividend and even calibrating to a more conservative gold price assumption (FY2026 estimates at $3,250/oz gold: ~$200 million) and a very conservative payout ratio of just 15% of free cash flow, investors could see $30 million returned or the equivalent of a US$0.20 annualized dividend.

Costs & Margins

As for costs and margins, Wesdome reported Q1'25 AISC of $1,528/oz, up 6% from $1,445/oz from the year-ago period. The increase in AISC was primarily related to the disappointing performance at Kiena, higher sustaining capital spend across the portfolio and higher royalties at Eagle River offset by strong performance at this Ontario mine. Fortunately, the record gold price still resulted in an increase in AISC margins to $1,751/oz (Q2 '24: $888/oz), translating to 53.4% AISC margins or a 1,500+ basis point increase year-over-year.

However, I wouldn't be shocked to see Wesdome's AISC dip back below $1,400/oz in 2026 with better performance out of Kiena, setting up further margin expansion next year assuming gold prices continue to hang out above $3,300/oz. And as noted previously, we should see much better AISC in Q4 '25 as Kiena expects to see its strongest quarter of the year.

The bigger opportunity here, though, is that both mines have considerable excess capacity to leverage their fixed cost base, and Wesdome could see a further improvement in costs medium-term if it were to grow into a 250,000 - 300,000 ounce producer. This is a work in progress, but Wesdome is one of the few names in the sector with the ability to grow organically with modest capex in a low-risk manner, with tapping into excess capacity being one of the more desirable avenues for growth vs. greenfields expansions that are much more expensive, are more complex on a relative basis and are at the mercy of new permits being issued.

Recent Developments

As for recent developments, the most recent was the announcement of Wesdome's Independent Director, Philip Yee, as its new CFO. Philip Yee was previously at Eldorado Gold (EGO) from 2018 to 2024 as CFO. Prior to that, he spent two years as EVP and CFO at Kirkland Lake Gold and three years at Lakeshore Gold as SVP and CFO prior to its sale to Pan American Silver (PAAS). Philip Yee will replace Raj Gill as interim CFO, with the new position set to begin on September 29th.

Eagle River Exploration

As for other developments, Wesdome continues to see exploration success at Eagle River. This included extending the 6 Central Zone by 300 meters to 600 meters down-plunge, with highlight intercepts of 1.6 meters (true width) at 115.9 G/T of gold, 2.0 meters (true width) at 49.1 G/T of gold and 3.2 meters (true width) at 47.0 G/T of gold. Wesdome noted that drilling is closing the gap towards 758-E-500, a high-grade intercept that hit 40.0 G/T of gold over 6.3 meters (true width) and which was excluded from the year-end 2024 resource update.

As it stands, Eagle River's reserves sit at ~487,000 ounces at 12.3 G/T of gold, which supports roughly 5 years of mine life, but the addition of considerable regional upside, the up-plunge potential of the 720 Falcon Zone near surface and the confirmation of the geological model at the 300 F zone as a separate structure from the 300 Zone are certainly very encouraging developments. Combined with considerable land added in the Angus Gold acquisition, including Dorset, and further potential at Mishi/Magnacon with Wesdome planning to test the down plunge extend of Mishi below its open-pit, there's certainly reason to be very optimistic about resource growth and resource conversion going forward.

And, like Kiena, Eagle River is operating at just half of its permitted capacity of 1,200 TPD, suggesting opportunity to push production higher at this mine as well longer-term. Hence, it's not just Kiena that has production upside, though Kiena offers the most leverage from a production upside standpoint given that it's using just one-third of permitted capacity currently at Kiena Deep (will increase to ~60% once Presqu'ile online).

Notably, Wesdome already owns two of the highest-grade gold mines in top-ranked jurisdictions, with Eagle River and Kiena highlighted below relative to other underground projects/mines based on reserves and reserve grades.

Labor

On a negative note, Wesdome wasn't shy about labor being a "major challenge." This is not surprising given the considerable growth by producers in the region like Agnico Eagle (AEM) and others, with Agnico alone looking to grow production by nearly 1.0 million ounces combined over the next several years (DL-UG - ~300,000 ounces, Upper Beaver - ~200,000 ounces, Canadian Malartic Complex - ~400,000 ounces). So, while Wesdome has ambitions to materially expand production, the lack of skilled labor could pose a challenge, especially with competition for labor only heating up in a very favorable gold price environment and in a prolific region.

Valuation

Based on ~154 million fully diluted shares and a share price of US$15.90, Wesdome trades at a market cap of ~$2.45 billion and an enterprise value of ~$2.31 billion. This continues to be a very reasonable valuation for a ~200,000 ounce producer with high-grade mines in top-ranked jurisdictions, and a far more palatable valuation than the ~$10 billion for Coeur Mining (CDE) in less favorable jurisdictions with a track record of per share destruction vs. Wesdome's track record of per share growth.

In fact, despite Wesdome sitting at all-time highs, the stock trades at a very reasonable valuation at less than ~10x FY2026 EV/FCF estimates ($3,350/oz gold) and just over 7.6x FY2026 EV/FCF estimates at a $3,600/oz gold price. for a dual-asset producer with organic growth potential.

And if we look out three years and assume Wesdome can grow into a 260,000 ounce producer by leveraging excess mill capacity, Wesdome trades at just ~3.7x FY2028 EV/FCF (flat $3,400/oz gold price in 2026-2028 period), making it one of the more interesting buy-the-dip candidates if we do see a decent pullback.

So, what's a fair value for the stock?

Using what I believe to be fair multiples of 9.0x cash flow and 1.2x P/NAV and a 7% discount rate with gold price assumptions of $3,350/oz (2026), $3,000/oz (2027) and $2,700/oz long-term, I see a fair value for the stock of US$17.90. This points to an 11% upside from current levels, but I am looking for at least a 27% discount to fair value for Tier-1 only producers of this scale to ensure a margin of safety. Hence, while I think Wesdome is a solid story with a promising long-term outlook, I don't see a low-risk buying opportunity in the stock yet using my more conservative base case assumptions.

As for the technical picture, Wesdome is in the upper end of its projected support/resistance range, with short-term resistance at US$16.10 and upper support at US$11.70, with the stock extended short-term and vulnerable to a correction. That said, if we were to see a pullback into the lower portion of this range, I would consider starting a new position in the stock.

Summary

Wesdome had a tough H1 '25 at Kiena but should see improved results in the second half and will benefit from record gold prices. Notably, the company is a unique name in the sector given its considerable excess permitted mill capacity, giving it a low-risk and relatively low capex path to organic growth at its two key assets, similar to Agnico Eagle (AEM) and unlike many of its peers relying on new builds to increase production. That said, the stock is still outside of its ideal buy zone and is getting a little extended here above US$16.00/share. Hence, while I think Wesdome makes for a solid buy-the-dip candidate, I don't see any way to justify chasing the stock above US$16.00.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Plug Power: This Market Has No Fear (Technical Analysis, Downgrade)
data.description:
Thesis Summary

Plug Power Inc. (NASDAQ:PLUG) has rallied over 120% in the last six months.

While there have been some positive catalysts for the company, I believe this is symptomatic of a market that is not afraid of risk.

The right ingredients are in place for speculative names like Plug to keep running from here. But how much further can we go?

I lay out some targets based on technical analysis.

Plug Power’s Recent Rally

Plug has squeezed over $100 in the last few months, reigniting interest in this once highly followed stock.

At its peak in 2021, Plug traded at over $70. In the last few years, though, the company has failed to deliver on its promises, and it even had to restate its financials at one point.

However, it seems like the recent earnings may have actually reignited investor interest, at least enough to cause a short squeeze.

In Q2 2025, Plug updated investors on “Project Quantum Leap,” which will focus on margin recovery, operational streamlining, and better cash management.

In the last quarter, revenue grew 21% year-over-year to $174 million, and electrolyzer sales tripled to $45 million.

Gross margins improved from negative 92% a year ago to negative 31%, and management reiterated its target of achieving gross margin neutrality by Q4 and positive EBITDA in 2026.

In terms of the balance sheet, there’s $140 million in cash and $300 million in available debt capacity.

There are also hopes that recent U.S. tax credit legislation could accelerate electrolyzer adoption and strengthen customer economics.

The company is targeting $700 million in 2025 and recently delivered its first 100MW electrolyzer.

Plug Power Stock Performance Is An Indicator Of Market Euphoria

But if you ask me, Plug’s recent rally is more a symptom of market euphoria than any tangible fundamentals.

Of course, there’s been some “positive” news in the last few weeks, but this is a stock that ultimately trades based on momentum.

At the height of the 2021 rally, it reached over $70, but this was mostly led by speculation rather than anything else. As we can see below, the P/S ratio exploded to over 30.

This was a time of ample liquidity, with tight credit yields and investors searching for returns further out on the risk curve.

We’re seeing things play out similarly now as we begin a Fed cutting cycle, and possibly something much more dovish, with President Trump and his administration pushing the Fed in this direction.

  • Small caps have begun to outperform.

  • Chinese stocks are back in fashion.

  • Some Altcoins are beginning to outperform Bitcoin (BTC-USD).

  • And we are seeing short squeezes all over the place.

The Plug rally is simply the latest symptom of a market that is entering a euphoric stage.

This doesn’t mean a top is necessarily close. As I’ve mentioned, the Fed has just begun cutting, and we have an administration adamant on keeping the economy hot.

Plug Power: Technical Analysis

My point to PLUG investors and traders alike is to be very careful moving forward.

In my opinion, looking at catalysts and technical levels is more important for a stock like this than anything else.

Looking at the 4h chart, PLUG is now approaching its first real test, the $3 level, where it topped out weeks ago and also where it tipped out back in July and January.

From a momentum perspective, though, we have now cleared the key EMAs and have a MACD about to flip bullish.

Looking at the weekly chart, we can identify some longer-term support and resistance levels. Below us, the $1.8 level would be key to hold in any retracement to keep the bullish momentum.

In terms of upside, I do think Plug could target the 200-week EMA at $7.2, with room to turn into the 78.6% retracement level at $16.

If we could get back near $2, I’d consider going long, with plans to exit around $7.

Final Thoughts

Personally, I’ve been burned on Plug before, and it’s not a stock I’d ever own long-term. In my opinion, the current rally is more based on speculation and macro dynamics than on any real bullish fundamentals.

But this doesn't mean we can’t profit. I do think this is a stock that has the potential to triple from here as market euphoria continues to increase.

data.title: CVR Partners: Higher Prices And Expanded Production Will Improve The EPS Forecast
data.description:
Investment Thesis

The fundamental analysis of the market situation in the mineral fertilizer sector suggests that demand for these products will increase in 2026. This fact leads me to look for shares of companies that have strong competitive positions in their industry. Among them is the American manufacturer CVR Partners, LP (NYSE:UAN). Its subsidiaries are showing an increase in production capacity utilization against the backdrop of an 18% increase in the average selling price of products. This could increase the company's profits by 34% in 2025. Moreover, there are plans to increase production volumes, which, in parallel with the development of pricing policy, could increase sales of nitrogen fertilizers by +13.76%. The assessment of the fair value of CVR Partners shares allows us to determine their growth potential at +38.3% to $125.57 per share. Therefore, my recommendation is “Buy.”

CVR Partners Company Overview

CVR Partners LP operates in the mineral fertilizer industry, which is primarily driven by the need to ensure global consumer safety. The shortage of agricultural goods caused by various fundamental factors is driving increased demand for chemicals among agricultural producers. Such chemicals are used to increase the yield of agricultural land. The triggers include the war in Ukraine, population growth, disruption of food transport and logistics chains, global warming, and climate change. The current conditions for farming are getting worse, which means that extra fertilizer is needed to make sure crops reach their planned yield. CVR Partners focuses on making nitrogen fertilizers, which include ammonia and urea-ammonium fertilizers.

The company's competitive advantages include plants managed by CVR Partners. The most important subsidiary is Coffeyville Resources Nitrogen Fertilizers, LLC, which produces nitrogen fertilizers at a unique facility. The management says, “It's the only plant like this in North America that uses oil coke gasification in its production process to get hydrogen.” The subsidiaries also include East Dubuque Nitrogen Fertilizers, LLC, which makes nitrogen fertilizers using natural gas.

CVR Partners Financial Profile: Q2 2025 Data Suggests Upward EPS Estimates

The financial stability of CVR Partners is improving, as evidenced by the results of the quarterly report for the first half of 2025. "The second quarter showed an increase in net profit to $39 million, or $3.67 per share (EPS). EBITDA amounted to $67 million, and revenue showed an increase of 26.8%." I believe that the company's financial results will continue to improve. Among the main reasons for this is the forecast for the market conditions in the nitrogen fertilizer industry in 2026. Additionally, the low GAAP P/E ratio (TTM) of 10.91 (compared to the industry median of 24.42) contributes to the positive assessment of its investment attractiveness.

With CVR Partners' profits growing, the company's management has announced a record dividend payment starting in August 2023 ($3.89 per common share). This is a clear signal to investors that its shares are becoming more attractive. Current values are far from those of 5-10 years ago. But due to profit growth, the process of increasing payments may continue. Besides, CVR Partners' average dividend yield is 17.21% over the last 4 years. The industry median is only ~2% by comparison.

The Mineral Fertilizer Market Is Booming

In analyzing CVR Partners' prospects, it is worth noting that overall trends in the global mineral fertilizer market indicate that nutrient consumption is expected to grow to 205 million tons by 2025. This would exceed the previous record. Ammonia production capacity will likely increase by 4% annually from 2023 to 2025 (largely due to Russia and the US, with cheaper mineral fertilizer production costs). The volume of nitrogen consumption will also likely increase to 116 million tons in 2025. This signals an increase in planned production volumes, which means additional profits from the sale of mineral fertilizers by CVR Partners' subsidiaries.

As such, I believe that the nitrogen fertilizer market will be characterized by signs of a boom in the foreseeable future. In addition to the parallel growth in demand for products and their production, we are seeing an increase in prices, which is improving the financial results of manufacturers. Such drivers as the increase in the world's population, a decrease in agricultural land for arable farming, and the desire to increase agricultural efficiency in the Asia-Pacific region and Africa will create conditions for maintaining the previous average annual growth rate of the global nitrogen fertilizer market at 4-6%.

To be sure, the cost of production also affects the price of mineral fertilizers. Natural gas prices account for about 80% of the cost. The current trade policy and macroeconomic situation in the world are creating uncertainty, which may lead to further increases in energy prices. But balanced production capacity (replacing Russian gas with American liquefied natural gas) is making natural gas prices more stable, limiting their potential growth and making it easier to predict nitrogen fertilizer prices.

A previously anticipated energy crisis, which had been forecast since 2022, did not live up to the negative predictions of skeptical analysts. The current price situation on the hydrocarbon market provides a clear picture of the financial planning of nitrogen fertilizer producers' cost estimates. The volatility of natural gas prices has changed significantly from historically high and shocking levels in 2022 to noticeably lower ones. As the natural gas market keeps getting more stable in 2025, we can say that the energy crisis is over. This fact has a positive impact on the nitrogen fertilizer market, keeping up its growth and maintaining the growing demand from agricultural producers.

Geopolitics Creates Conditions for Business Development

The geopolitical backdrop is usually considered a trigger that creates risks and threats for business growth. But with CVR Partners, 2025 will continue to have a positive effect on boosting financial results. This is because the company is under US jurisdiction. CVR Partners has a good starting point and ample access to natural gas orders (while being subject to the same market pressures and volatility as all natural gas users). This supply of resources allows the company to maintain a stable production cycle, fulfilling orders on time and maintaining high production capacity utilization (around 91%). The continued instability in the European and Middle Eastern natural gas markets creates unfavorable conditions for nitrogen fertilizer producers in European countries (which have higher production costs due to natural gas prices and production interruptions) and the Middle East and North Africa (due to temporary disruptions in natural gas supplies to producers, especially in Egypt, which resulted in wasted time as many nitrogen fertilizer plants were idle).

Moreover, the crisis surrounding Ukraine, in which the Trump administration is increasingly dissatisfied with the negotiations with Putin, creates geopolitical risks of continued military conflict. This factor is important, first, because Russian gas is still not fully available to European producers. Previously, a diplomatic solution to the conflict was expected in 2025, which would have resumed natural gas supplies from Russia to European hubs. But this is unlikely to happen before 2026.

For instance, in spring 2025, the Polymarket forecasting website predicted a 78% probability of a diplomatic end to the war by the end of the year. This figure had fallen to 10% by October 1, 2025. As the conflict continues, practical solutions that would allow for negotiations on lifting sanctions on Russia's energy sector and resuming natural gas supplies to EU countries, even through Ukraine's gas transportation system, are being put on hold. In 2021, for example, Russian gas supplies amounted to 41.7 billion cubic meters.

Secondly, it is important to remember that it was the sanctions imposed on Russia in 2022 that led to the partial loss of access to Western markets for the largest producers of nitrogen fertilizers. As an example, we can take PhosAgro, the largest Russian producer of nitrogen fertilizers in the world. With an annual output of 11.8 million tons of mineral fertilizers, the company occupies a leading position in the global industry. From a total volume of 2.6 million tons, this is ammonia production. Considering that in 2024 the global market volume was 114 million tons, PhosAgro has a 2.3% share. A free flow of such a huge volume of mineral fertilizers into the European and American markets could lead to the opposite effect of the sanctions—a decrease in ammonia prices. For CVR Partners, this would mean a decline in profit growth. But as the geopolitical situation stays shaky and the news at the end of September brings some negative surprise, according to President Trump, the positive impact of geopolitics on business development is likely to continue.

The Valuation Shows +13.76% in Sales

Considering the rapid development of the modern mineral fertilizer industry, CVR Partners' management has decided to begin implementing plans to expand production capacity in order to increase ammonia production by 8%. For this purpose, an investment plan worth $55–65 million will be implemented, as high demand is creating reasons for an increase in product sales prices. The limited reserves in CVR Partners' warehouses do not allow for a significant increase in sales in the future without expanding the capacity for ammonia production.

The practical question arises as to how feasible CVR Partners' plans are and what income they can generate in the context of the boom in the mineral fertilizer market. The primary focus of production expansion is ammonia products. Let us assess how this decision will affect potential profits.

In Q2 2025, “a total of 197,000 tons of ammonia were produced, of which 54,000 tons net were available for sale, with the remainder being processed into other fertilizers, including 321,000 tons of urea-ammonia fertilizers.” Also, Q2 2025 saw an increase in the average selling price of ammonia and urea ammonium fertilizers by 14% and 18%, respectively, compared to Q2 2024 prices. The average selling price of mineral fertilizers is now $593 and $317, respectively. This results in CVR Partners selling $32.022 million worth of ammonia and $101.757 million worth of urea and ammonium fertilizers. Total sales of these products amount to $133.779 million.

Let's now look at CVR Partners' financial capabilities. Based on the results of the second quarter of 2025, the company has accumulated a cash balance of $114.4 million. Given that an 8% increase in ammonia production requires an average of $60 million, a 1% increase in production capacity costs $7.5 million. To implement its production expansion plan, the company could spend 52.44% of all its cash on the balance sheet. But if the company uses all the $100+ million available, production could grow by 15.25% instead of 8%. Of course, the company won't take this step right away and will use borrowed funds to increase production capacity. It should be understood that most of the cash is needed for other purposes.

Let us now proceed to the valuation itself. Considering that CVR Partners will spend an average of $60 million on expanding production, the effect of increasing the volume of ammonia produced will be 8% or about 213,000 tons in real terms. According to the company's distribution of ammonia production between ammonia sales and the production of urea-ammonia fertilizers, the ratio is 27% to 73%. The result is that of the 8% increase in production, 2.16% will go to sales of ammonia itself and 5.84% to the production of urea-ammonia fertilizers. As such, ammonia sales will increase from 54,000 tons to 55,166 tons, while urea-ammonia fertilizer sales will increase from 321,000 tons to 339,746 tons.

Considering that over the past 12 months, prices for ammonia and urea-ammonium fertilizers have risen by 14% and 18%, respectively, I think that in the next six months, these increases may continue at the same pace and reach a median of 7% and 9%. On this basis, the selling prices of the products will be $634 and $345, respectively.

It should be noted right away that forecasting ammonia price growth in the second half of 2025 is fraught with difficulties. This is due to the influence of several groups of factors. The greater the number of triggers affecting nitrogen fertilizer pricing, the more difficult it is to predict their price in the foreseeable future. Some of the most important factors include supply in Western markets, where Russian producers still have a minimal presence. Other key factors include changes in inflation, market volatility in natural gas prices, and foreign trade policy, which could lead to additional customs tariffs.

Nevertheless, the consensus is that nitrogen fertilizer prices will continue to grow at the same pace. This is primarily due to a lack of confidence that imports of Russian fertilizers will return to Western mineral fertilizer markets in full by the end of 2025. In addition, inflationary risks remain. Analysts predict that the next US inflation figures will probably remain at the previous level of +2.9%. The inflationary trend is driving up food prices. It is directly linked to rising prices for agricultural products, whose producers are consumers of nitrogen fertilizers.

The additional introduction of tariffs by the European Union on imports of mineral fertilizers from Russia should be considered an additional factor. The cost of the additional tariff for Russian ammonia will be $40-45 per ton of fertilizer. These updates happened in July 2025 and only affected the price of nitrogen fertilizers starting in the third quarter. Among other things, pushing up ammonia prices is growing demand from farmers in India and Brazil.

As such, CVR Partners' sales of ammonia and urea-ammonia fertilizers, after implementing plans to expand production by 8%, will amount to $34.975 million and $117.212 million. Total sales will amount to $152.187 million. A final increase in sales from $133.779 million to $152.187 million will represent a growth of +13.76%.

Risks to the Investment Thesis

Studying CVR Partners' financial profile and business strategy has led me to a positive assessment of the investment attractiveness of their shares. Meanwhile, the growth in chemical sales prices will most likely continue to be supported by market conditions in the mineral fertilizer sector. Nevertheless, CVR Partners' business model is subject to a number of risks that could affect the further growth of its share price.

Although CVR Partners' investment attractiveness is viewed positively, its business model is subject to a number of risks. Geopolitical factors pose the most significant risks, which are currently creating conditions for nitrogen fertilizer prices to rise. The problem of ensuring the security of global supply chains is of secondary importance for CVR Partners, as production is resource-supported by domestic US gas and the main sales of products are in North America. At the same time, however, a significant improvement in the geopolitical risk situation could have the opposite effect, leading to a decline in nitrogen fertilizer prices (as the market will see an increase in the supply of mineral fertilizers from producers, primarily players such as PhosAgro). In addition, the continued imposition of sanctions on Russian companies creates an additional unpredictable factor that will significantly affect pricing in the near term.

Further factors negatively impacting CVR Partners' profit growth prospects include potential increases in natural gas prices and changes in electricity costs in the US states. But the impact of these problems isn't new, and it always seems more serious in winter. U.S. spot natural gas prices over the past couple of years are shown in the figure below. They represent stable price dynamics. In the event of a change in weather conditions and a reorientation of US liquefied gas producers towards the European market, however, conditions may be created for price increases within the US.

Stock Forecast and Targets

I used a DCF model for my valuation. It will yield comparative results, which allow us to determine the level of investment attractiveness. Instead of FCFF, I will use the distributable cash flow indicator to assess the fair value of CVR Partners.

For the projection part of the DCF model, I have chosen the nearest 5-year period from 2025 to 2029. The key valuation tool is the discount rate. The WACC rate used in my calculations is approximately 7.9%. CVR Partners' expected profit growth in the forecast period will range from 34% in 2025 to 4% in 2029. This rapid profit growth in 2025 is due to the results of the first half of the year. Q1 EBITDA was $53 million, and Q2 EBITDA was $67 million. This brings CVR Partners' EBITDA for the first half of 2025 to $120 million. Assuming that the second half of 2025 maintains the same profit growth rate, total EBITDA will be $240 million. Continued profit growth is driven by economic conditions in the mineral fertilizer market. The company forecasts profit growth for the entire mineral fertilizer sector over the next three years at 21.2%. The table below shows the free cash flow chart for CVR Partners, excluding borrowed funds.
